page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The processor features a 32 KB L1 data cache and 32 MB of L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA. This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. The c2d-standard-2 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2923.48. However, its multi-core CPU performance is categorized as weak, falling into the bottom 25% with a score of 2928.18, which reflects the physical limitations of its single-core, dual-thread allocation. Memory performance is constrained, with cached small-block read bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10% at 136.13 GB/sec, and uncached large-block read and write bandwidths both ranking in the bottom 25%. Geekbench testing aligns with these findings, showing average single-core and text processing capabilities but poor multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-standard-2 offers a dedicated AMD EPYC hardware profile suitable for entry-level compute tasks without the overhead of larger instances. However, resource density tradeoffs are evident in database and web-serving workloads. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks both rank in the bottom 10%, with Redis achieving 2.22 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also limited to 2.82 GB/sec. Consequently, this instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, or small-scale LLM text generation tasks using compact models, rather than high-throughput databases, parallelized compilation, or memory-intensive services.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is an entry-level compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It operates with 1 physical core and 2 threads, supported by a 32 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Conversely, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower percentiles due to the single-core hardware constraint. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for basic single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight utility services, while presenting clear performance tradeoffs for multi-threaded compilation, high-throughput web serving, or database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is a compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 features 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
tagline: An entry-level dedicated AMD EPYC instance delivering strong single-core execution for lightweight, compute-focused workloads with a balanced memory footprint.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 1 physical core
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, text processing, and basic utility services
